summary: Extended Path Map Node Intel with related trail-event stacks so a focused route node reveals nearby service requests, traces, outcomes, and evidence without leaving the route board.

queries: timeline detail panel related events UX dashboard drilldown
activity timeline detail pane design pattern dashboard
roadmap milestone details activity feed panel UX
pattern_notes: Timeline drilldowns work best when selected detail stays near the overview and keeps the user's spatial context.
Activity feeds should make event type, time, title, and short summary scannable before asking the operator to open a deeper record.
Roadmap and dashboard detail panels commonly pair a selected milestone with related events so blockers and proof are visible in one place.

ui_contract: Score related trail records from the focused node's stable id, kind, status, title, meta, body, and local note text.
Show up to three related records with event type, timestamp, title, and summary inside Node Intel.
Fall back to the newest trail records when an exact match is unavailable so new lanes still have useful context.
Add a direct TRL action from Node Intel to the full lane trail.
Keep all state and matching browser-local or snapshot-derived; do not mutate lane data or contact agents.

red_route.evidence: Before implementation, .path-intel-events was absent, .path-intel-event count was 0, and Node Intel had no data-detail-view="trail" action.

green_route.evidence: The focused gate rendered a related event stack with 3 records and a TRL button; clicking Startup Memo changed the Node Intel title and first related event to a trace-focused record. No console errors, failed requests, or bad rectangles were reported.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered 3 related event records in Node Intel with document width equal to viewport width and no overflow or bad rectangles.
